title: Typed API client, WebSocket hook, and Zustand store
acceptance_criteria: frontend/src/types/api.ts defines TypeScript interfaces for all API response shapes: PersonalityConfig, PaperTrade, StraddleSignal, StraddleSnapshot, RetrospectionResult, EvolutionApproval, WsMessage (union type)

frontend/src/lib/api.ts exports typed async functions: getPersonalities(), getPersonality(id), getTrades(params), getTradeById(id), getSignals(params), getStraddleLatest(), getStraddleHistory(params), getRetrospection(params), getPendingEvolutions(), approveEvolution(id), rejectEvolution(id, reason?); all use Axios base client pointing to /api (relative URL, works through Vite proxy in dev and same-origin in prod)

frontend/src/lib/ws.ts exports useWebSocket() hook that: derives WS URL from window.location (ws:// or wss:// + host + /ws/stream), connects on mount, reconnects with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 30s cap), exposes { lastMessage, connectionStatus: 'connecting'|'connected'|'disconnected' }

frontend/src/store/trading.ts Zustand store with: personalities: PersonalityConfig[], recentSignals: StraddleSignal[] (last 20), straddleHistory: StraddleSnapshot[], openTrades: PaperTrade[], systemStatus: { mode: string, underlying: string, connected: boolean }; WS messages update the appropriate slice: snapshot → straddleHistory.push(), signal → recentSignals.unshift().slice(0,20), trade_open → openTrades.push(), trade_close → openTrades filter out closed
App.tsx wraps with QueryClientProvider and initialises the WS hook
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
